title: UV Crosslinking of Suspension Cells for eCLIP
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"6gk2e","text":"Profiling of RNA binding protein targets in vivo provides critical insights into the mechanistic roles they play in regulating RNA processing. The enhanced crosslinking and immunoprecipitation (eCLIP) methodology provides a framework for robust, reproducible identification of transcriptome-wide protein-RNA interactions, with dramatically improved efficiency over previous methods. Here we provide a step-by-step description for UV crosslinking of suspension cells to prepare them for eCLIP experiments.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"agq96","text":"Cell viability validation (prior to crosslinking):","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":50}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9un55","text":"a. Use Trypan blue stain (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Cat# 15250-061) or other equivalent live cell counting assay to assay cell viability","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3af4s","text":"b. Cell viability should be \u003e 95% to ensure intact RNA","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"7o888","text":"Required materials:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":19}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4k4u9","text":" UV crosslinker with 254-nmwavelength UV bulbs (UVP CL-1000 Ultraviolet Crosslinker or equivalent)","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":21,"length":6},{"style":"italic","offset":47,"length":50}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"144fk","text":" Liquid nitrogen (sufficient to submerge tubes in appropriate container)","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c9jn7","text":" 1x DPBS (Corning cat #21-031-CV or equivalent)","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bp2r7","text":" Trypan blue stain (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Cat# 15250-061 or other equivalent live cell counting assay)","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":20,"length":85}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bdddo","text":" Standard cell counting system (hemocytometer or automated cell counter)","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6hj7m","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Aliquot at most 60×10 6 cells (re-suspended in 1x DPBS) in at least 3 mL total volume to a standard 10cm tissue culture grade plate. Note : Ensure the cells are evenly dispersed and the plate is fully covered (3 mL should be sufficient volume).
Place the above (plate plus ice or cooling block) into the UV cross-linker. Note: Ensure the plate is leveled. Remove tissue culture plate lid for cross-linking.
After crosslinking is completed, transfer the cells to a 50 mL conical tube
Resuspend the pellet(s) in 25 mL of 1x DPBS at room temperature.
Spin down remaining sample in 50ml conical tube(s) at 200g for 5 minutes at room temperature.
Count cell concentration (either with automated cell counter or hemocytometer)
Aspirate supernatant
Wash plate once with 7mL of 1x DPBS and add to the same 50 mL tube.
Cross-link at 254-nm UV with an energy setting of 400 mJoules/cm 2 Note: this is a setting of 4000 on many cross-linkers which display values in 0.1 mJoules/cm 2
Gently resuspend until the sample is homogeneous
Count cell concentration (either with automated cell counter or hemocytometer) Note : ensure cells are re-suspended well before counting
Resuspend in the desired amount for flash freezing Typically 20×10 6 cells per mL.
Aspirate and discard supernatant.
Centrifuge the 50 ml conical tube at 200g for five minutes at room temperature.
After frozen (at least thirty seconds), remove from the liquid nitrogen and store at -80°C.
Aspirate the supernatant and freeze by submerging the epi-tubes completely in liquid nitrogen.
Spin down at 200g for five minutes at room temperature.
Transfer desired amount into 1.5 mL Eppendorf Safe-Lock Tubes (or equivalent) Typically 1mL of 20×10 6 cells per mL.
Pool all cells per biosample (if multiple plates)
Aspirate spent media
Centrifuge at 200g for 5 minutes at room temperature
Transfer cells with media to 50mL conical tube(s)
Resuspend cells to no more than 20×10 6 cells per mL
Place the tissue culture plate on leveled ice or a cooling block pre-chilled to 4°C
creator: Eric Van Nostrand
